reviews.text: I am very disappointed with this clinic. We chose it because of the many positive reviews, but in reality, it turned out to be the worst experience. When we made the booking, the receptionist was helpful. However, after the initial consultation, they were not supportive at all. Their communication was vague, with no clear explanations or treatment plans. It was just a waste of money and time. Were all those reviews fake…?
Dr wattar sold us a dream. Unfortunately lost over 20k without results.

Seeing as Evewell have responded (for once) I thought I would update my review.

To clarify I meant we were quoted two incorrect figures for two different services. There justification is a few days later they send through the correct price with no explanation or even apology.

Also we did not leave due to the money factor. We stopped using them due to regularly making mistakes, slow response time, some doctors showing no compassion or having any time for you, and then chasing you to start IVF.

We went for couples fertility tests and really would not recommend this establishment.

From the offset they kept writing we had opted to go for IVF which came across as very pushy.

On two occasions we were quoted one price and had to pay a very difference price e.g. £428 then having to pay £674

On one occasion they knew I was going for a test they had referred me to another clinic TDL. I did not have the correct forms from Evewell even though for weeks I had known I was going. I had to go to their Harley st practice (which we are not clients) and plead for the correct forms as I had specifically travelled back to the UK from Paris due to work to do this test!

To top things off they even quoted the incorrect tests I needed to go for. So it's not just the money factor. They have extremely slow response times, expect you to swallow their mistakes, and then pressure you to feel behind when half the time it is their poor management / organisation.

On one occasion my partner saw another Doctor for a test and this person was extremely cold, had no time to explain anything, and said to talk to your normal Doctor.

You have to chase for your results and then in the next breath they tell you that you are behind and need to start IVF asap.

So overall extremely poorly organised, make you pay for their costly mistakes, and as a result we will be pursuing our treatment elsewhere

My partner and I went to The Evewell Clinic for secondary infertility and, now that our journey has ended, I feel compelled to share our mixed experience. We entered IVF knowing nothing and were completely unprepared for how painful and overwhelming it would be.

The nursing team was the highlight of the clinic—kind, patient, and genuinely supportive. They explained things clearly and offered emotional reassurance when we needed it most. Unfortunately, this level of care didn’t extend to the rest of the clinic. The administrative and finance staff often lacked empathy and organisation, and their communication felt cold and dismissive at times. For a process as emotionally heavy as IVF, this really mattered.

Dr. Eva was pleasant, but difficult to reach, and important information wasn’t communicated clearly. We often felt left in the dark, especially about risks, statistics, and what to expect. Messages went through a central team, and replies were slow or never came. This added a lot of unnecessary stress.

Costs were another major issue. We repeatedly asked for clarity but were only given a price list full of jargon we didn’t understand. No one explained what an average patient might expect to pay. We also later discovered we could have bought medication far cheaper elsewhere—something the clinic never mentioned, even though the difference was over £1,000 in our case. It left us feeling taken advantage of at a time when we were vulnerable.

Across two IVF cycles, we created 13 embryos and ended up with two viable ones after PGTA testing. Our first transfer failed. The second embryo was lost during thawing—something with a 99% success rate. We were devastated and unconvinced by the vague explanation of “it happens sometimes.” We are still convinced it was human error. After that, they suggested ovulation induction, which was poorly managed and far more expensive than implied.

What hurt most was the lack of guidance. We weren’t told early on that, at our age, donor eggs might be a realistic consideration—information that could have helped us prepare emotionally. They only discussed it after our second failure, and even admitted they lose patients because they don’t offer donor services. It felt like information was withheld simply because it didn’t benefit the clinic.

By the end, we felt abandoned. No aftercare, no follow‑up, no support—just silence. Not even a sales pitch. After everything we went through, that was incredibly painful.

I’m sure there are better clinics out there, and I hope others have a kinder, more transparent experience than we did.

The staff were very friendly but unfortunately they didn’t deliver. We attended an initial screening appointment and a consultation. This was our first clinic we attended and the consultation didn’t address the main issue we had and didn’t …

Great clinicians and very pleased with the consultations I had. Sadly the patient support/ front office team let down the experience - had to chase for appointments, follow ups and to get my correct test results after submitting a data request for my medical records. I get the impression that if we’d paid for IVF up front and weren’t having investigations paid for by my insurance we would have had a much better experience. We have chosen another clinic for our IVF cycles because of this (after radio silence about my IVF consultation appointment questions).

The Evewell offered a great service during treatment; staff are supportive and friendly and put you at ease. Sadly after an embryo transfer and a negative pregnancy test there was no follow up after care, except for on the day of the test contact by the nurse. Given this is a private clinic my expectations for after care was much higher.
